description: Need a quick rsync wrapper at /home/user/bin/sync-docs.sh that pulls everything from /home/user/remote-docs/ into /home/user/local-docs/, preserving timestamps and permissions. Should skip any file larger than 50MB though — we've got some video dumps in there that don't need to sync. Make it executable.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/bin/ exists and is writable.
- /home/user/bin/sync-docs.sh does not exist.
- /home/user/remote-docs/ exists with the following structure:
    - notes.txt (1KB, mtime 2024-01-15 10:00:00, mode 644)
    - report.pdf (2MB, mtime 2024-02-20 14:30:00, mode 640)
    - slides.pptx (8MB, mtime 2024-03-01 09:15:00, mode 644)
    - training-video.mp4 (120MB, mtime 2024-03-05 16:00:00, mode 644)
    - archive/old-notes.txt (500B, mtime 2023-06-10 08:00:00, mode 644)
    - archive/demo-recording.mov (75MB, mtime 2023-07-22 11:30:00, mode 644)
- /home/user/local-docs/ exists and is empty.
- rsync is installed.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/bin/sync-docs.sh exists and is executable (mode includes at least u+x).
- Running `/home/user/bin/sync-docs.sh` exits 0.
- After running the script, /home/user/local-docs/ contains:
    - notes.txt (same content, mtime preserved, permissions preserved)
    - report.pdf (same content, mtime preserved, permissions preserved)
    - slides.pptx (same content, mtime preserved, permissions preserved)
    - archive/old-notes.txt (same content, mtime preserved, permissions preserved)
- /home/user/local-docs/ does NOT contain training-video.mp4 or archive/demo-recording.mov (both exceed 50MB).

Invariants:
- /home/user/remote-docs/ and its contents unchanged.
- No files outside /home/user/local-docs/ and /home/user/bin/ modified.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The script must use rsync with --max-size (or equivalent filtering) — grep the script for 'rsync' and for a size limit flag/option. A script that manually copies files without rsync or hardcodes specific filenames to exclude does not satisfy the task.
- Running the script a second time should be idempotent (rsync's normal behavior).
